name: Athletic Training
description: Introduces the profession of athletic training, covering injury prevention, evaluation, first aid, taping, rehabilitation, and career pathways. Students gain hands-on knowledge of caring for the physically active.

modules.title: Introduction to Athletic Training
modules.objectives: Define athletic training as a healthcare profession; describe the certified athletic trainer (ATC) role; identify diverse work settings.
modules.videoNote: Day in the Life of an Athletic Trainer
modules.assignment: Interview or shadow a certified athletic trainer. Write a one-page summary of their daily responsibilities, work setting, and career path.

modules.title: Anatomy for Athletic Trainers
modules.objectives: Identify major bones, joints, muscles, and ligaments relevant to sport injury; use correct anatomical terminology for location and movement.
modules.readingNote: Khan Academy — Human Anatomy and Physiology
modules.assignment: Label a full musculoskeletal diagram identifying 20 key structures. Write a brief description of each structure's role in athletic movement.

modules.title: Injury Prevention Strategies
modules.objectives: Identify intrinsic and extrinsic risk factors for sport injury; explain how warm-up, conditioning, protective equipment, and rule enforcement reduce risk.
modules.assignment: Select one sport and create a comprehensive injury prevention protocol including pre-season conditioning, warm-up routines, and equipment guidelines.

modules.title: First Aid and Emergency Procedures
modules.objectives: Describe Emergency Action Plan (EAP) components; demonstrate first aid for common on-field emergencies; explain criteria for activating EMS.
modules.readingNote: American Red Cross — First Aid Basics
modules.videoNote: First Aid Basics for Sports Emergencies
modules.practiceNote: Red Cross — Online First Aid Simulation
modules.assignment: Draft a written Emergency Action Plan (EAP) for a high school athletic event. Include roles, communication steps, and emergency equipment locations.

modules.title: Taping and Bracing Techniques
modules.objectives: Explain purposes of prophylactic taping and bracing; describe ankle and wrist taping procedures; distinguish functional from prophylactic braces.
modules.videoNote: Ankle Taping Technique Step by Step
modules.assignment: Practice and document an ankle taping procedure on a partner using proper pre-wrap and tape layers. Write a self-critique of your technique.

modules.title: Rehabilitation Principles
modules.objectives: Describe phases of injury rehabilitation; explain PRICE protocol and progressive loading; identify evidence-based return-to-play criteria.
modules.assignment: Design a 4-week rehabilitation plan for a Grade II ankle sprain. Include phase goals, exercises, progression criteria, and return-to-play standards.

modules.title: Common Sports Injuries
modules.objectives: Identify mechanism, signs, symptoms, and initial management of the most common sport injuries by body region.
modules.assignment: Create a one-page reference card for 5 common sport injuries, including mechanism, signs/symptoms, field management, and referral criteria for each.

modules.title: Concussion Management
modules.objectives: Define concussion; identify signs and symptoms; explain CDC Heads Up protocols and the graduated return-to-play and return-to-learn protocol.
modules.readingNote: CDC Heads Up — Concussion Signs and Symptoms
modules.videoNote: Concussion: What You Need to Know
modules.practiceNote: CDC Heads Up — Coaches Concussion Training
modules.assignment: Complete the CDC Heads Up online concussion training. Write a policy recommendation for your school's concussion management protocol.

quizQuestions.question: The professional credential for a certified athletic trainer is:
quizQuestions.explanation: ATC (Athletic Trainer Certified) is awarded by the Board of Certification to qualified athletic trainers.

quizQuestions.question: Athletic trainers primarily work with:
options: Physically active individuals across all ages and settings
quizQuestions.answer: Physically active individuals across all ages and settings
quizQuestions.explanation: Certified athletic trainers work in high schools, colleges, professional sports, military, clinics, and performing arts.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The professional credential for a certified athletic trainer is:" is: ATC.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. ATC is correct.

quizQuestions.question: The anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) is located in the:
quizQuestions.explanation: The ACL is a major stabilizing ligament inside the knee joint, connecting femur to tibia.

quizQuestions.question: A sprain involves injury to:
quizQuestions.explanation: Sprains are ligament injuries (bone-to-bone), while strains involve muscles or tendons.

quizQuestions.question: The rotator cuff is a group of muscles and tendons surrounding the:
quizQuestions.explanation: The rotator cuff (supraspinatus, infraspinatus, teres minor, subscapularis) stabilizes the glenohumeral joint.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) is located in the:" is: Knee.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Knee is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Intrinsic risk factors for sport injury include:
options: Previous injury history and muscle imbalances
quizQuestions.answer: Previous injury history and muscle imbalances
quizQuestions.explanation: Intrinsic factors are internal athlete characteristics influencing injury susceptibility.

quizQuestions.question: Neuromuscular training programs like FIFA 11+ primarily prevent:
quizQuestions.explanation: FIFA 11+ and similar programs target landing mechanics and hip/core strength to reduce ACL injury rates.

quizQuestions.question: The most effective prevention strategy for overuse injuries is:
options: Gradually increasing training load with adequate recovery
quizQuestions.answer: Gradually increasing training load with adequate recovery
quizQuestions.explanation: Overuse injuries result from repetitive microtrauma exceeding tissue repair; progressive load management prevents them.

quizQuestions.question: The primary goal of an Emergency Action Plan (EAP) is to:
options: Ensure rapid, coordinated response to athletic emergencies
quizQuestions.answer: Ensure rapid, coordinated response to athletic emergencies
quizQuestions.explanation: An EAP outlines roles, communication, and equipment to enable a fast, organized emergency response.

quizQuestions.question: Signs of exertional heat stroke include:
options: Hot, dry skin and altered mental status
Low heart rate and normal temperature
quizQuestions.answer: Hot, dry skin and altered mental status
quizQuestions.explanation: Heat stroke is a life-threatening emergency: hyperthermia, cessation of sweating, and CNS dysfunction.

quizQuestions.question: Recommended first aid for a suspected spinal injury is:
options: Have the athlete walk off the field
Stabilize the head and neck; do not move the athlete
Apply ice and return to play
quizQuestions.answer: Stabilize the head and neck; do not move the athlete
quizQuestions.explanation: Suspected spinal injuries require immobilization — movement without proper precautions risks permanent paralysis.

quizQuestions.question: AED stands for:
quizQuestions.explanation: An AED delivers an electrical shock to restore normal heart rhythm during sudden cardiac arrest.

quizQuestions.question: Prophylactic ankle taping primarily prevents:
options: Ankle sprains by limiting excessive inversion
quizQuestions.answer: Ankle sprains by limiting excessive inversion
quizQuestions.explanation: Prophylactic taping restricts excessive inversion motion, preventing lateral ankle sprains.

quizQuestions.question: Pre-wrap is applied before athletic tape to:
quizQuestions.explanation: Pre-wrap creates a protective barrier between the skin and rigid athletic tape.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Prophylactic ankle taping primarily prevents:" is: Ankle sprains by limiting excessive inversion.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Ankle sprains by limiting excessive inversion is correct.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Pre-wrap is applied before athletic tape to:" is: Add compression.
quizQuestions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Protect skin from adhesive.

quizQuestions.question: The acute inflammatory phase of injury healing lasts approximately:
quizQuestions.explanation: Acute inflammation begins immediately after injury and typically resolves within 72 hours.

quizQuestions.question: During the proliferative phase of healing, the primary tissue deposited is:
quizQuestions.explanation: Fibroblasts lay down type III collagen forming immature scar tissue during the proliferative phase (days 3–21).

quizQuestions.question: Progressive loading in rehabilitation means:
options: Systematically increasing exercise demand as tissue heals
Avoiding all exercise until fully healed
quizQuestions.answer: Systematically increasing exercise demand as tissue heals
quizQuestions.explanation: Progressive loading stimulates tissue remodeling without exceeding the healing tissue's tolerance.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The acute inflammatory phase of injury healing lasts approximately:" is: 0–72 hours post-injury.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. 0–72 hours post-injury is correct.

quizQuestions.question: A Grade II ankle sprain involves:
options: Partial ligament tear with moderate pain and swelling
Complete ligament rupture with severe instability
quizQuestions.answer: Partial ligament tear with moderate pain and swelling
quizQuestions.explanation: Grade II sprains involve partial ligament tearing with moderate pain, swelling, and some instability.

quizQuestions.question: A concussion is classified as:
quizQuestions.explanation: A concussion is a mild TBI caused by biomechanical forces that temporarily alter brain function.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "A Grade II ankle sprain involves:" is: Partial ligament tear with moderate pain and swelling.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Partial ligament tear with moderate pain and swelling is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Concussion is diagnosed primarily by:
options: Clinical evaluation of signs and symptoms
quizQuestions.answer: Clinical evaluation of signs and symptoms
quizQuestions.explanation: Standard neuroimaging appears normal in most concussions; diagnosis is clinical.

quizQuestions.question: CDC's 'When in Doubt, Sit Them Out' means:
options: Rest only if the athlete loses consciousness
Remove any suspected concussion athlete from play immediately
Athletes may return after 15 minutes if symptoms resolve
quizQuestions.answer: Remove any suspected concussion athlete from play immediately
quizQuestions.explanation: The CDC recommends immediate removal to prevent second-impact syndrome.

quizQuestions.question: Second-impact syndrome occurs when:
options: An athlete sustains two concussions years apart
A second head injury occurs before full recovery from the first concussion
An athlete is concussed twice in the same game with no symptoms
quizQuestions.answer: A second head injury occurs before full recovery from the first concussion
quizQuestions.explanation: Second-impact syndrome can cause rapid, catastrophic brain swelling and is potentially fatal.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Concussion is diagnosed primarily by:" is: Clinical evaluation of signs and symptoms.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Clinical evaluation of signs and symptoms is correct.

questions.question: The professional credential for a certified athletic trainer is:
questions.explanation: ATC is awarded by the Board of Certification to qualified athletic trainers.

questions.question: A sprain involves injury to:
questions.explanation: Sprains are ligament injuries; strains involve muscles or tendons.

questions.question: The ACL is located in the:
questions.explanation: The anterior cruciate ligament is a major stabilizing ligament inside the knee joint.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which best prevents overuse injuries" is: Gradually increasing load with adequate recovery.
questions.explanation: True. Gradually increasing load with adequate recovery is correct.

questions.question: AED stands for:
questions.explanation: An AED delivers electrical shock to restore normal heart rhythm during cardiac arrest.

questions.question: Pre-wrap is used before athletic tape to:
questions.explanation: Pre-wrap creates a protective barrier between skin and rigid tape.

questions.question: The acute inflammatory phase of healing lasts approximately:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: 0–72 hours.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "DOMS peaks approximately:" is: Immediately during exercise.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: 24–72 hours after exercise.

questions.question: Concussion is most accurately diagnosed by:
options: Clinical evaluation of signs and symptoms
questions.answer: Clinical evaluation of signs and symptoms
questions.explanation: Standard imaging appears normal in most concussions; diagnosis is clinical.

questions.question: Carbohydrate loading before endurance events aims to:
questions.explanation: Carbohydrate loading saturates glycogen stores to delay fatigue during endurance events.

questions.question: Periodization divides training into:
questions.explanation: Periodization uses nested training cycles to manage volume and intensity across a season.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "WADA is responsible for:" is: International anti-doping regulation and monitoring.
questions.explanation: True. International anti-doping regulation and monitoring is correct.

questions.question: Informed consent in sports medicine means:
options: Athletes consent automatically by joining a team
Athletes agree to treatment after understanding risks and alternatives
Emergency treatment always requires prior consent
questions.answer: Athletes agree to treatment after understanding risks and alternatives
questions.explanation: Informed consent requires clear communication of treatment options, risks, and benefits before agreement.

questions.question: SOAP notes stand for:
questions.explanation: SOAP is a standard healthcare documentation format.

questions.question: The Achilles tendon connects to the:
questions.explanation: The Achilles tendon attaches the gastrocnemius and soleus to the heel bone.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "HOPS stands for:" is: Heat, Observation, Pressure, Splinting.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: History, Observation, Palpation, Special tests.

questions.question: A positive Lachman test indicates injury to the:
questions.explanation: The Lachman test is the most sensitive clinical test for ACL rupture.

questions.question: Second-impact syndrome occurs when:
options: A second head injury occurs before full recovery from the first
An athlete is hit twice with no symptoms
questions.answer: A second head injury occurs before full recovery from the first
questions.explanation: Second-impact syndrome can cause rapid, catastrophic brain swelling and is potentially fatal.

questions.question: The rotator cuff surrounds the:
questions.explanation: The four rotator cuff muscles stabilize the glenohumeral (shoulder) joint.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "PRICE adds which step to RICE" is: Protection.
questions.explanation: True. Protection is correct.

questions.question: Entry-level ATs must graduate from:
questions.explanation: Since 2022, entry-level AT practice requires a CAATE-accredited master's degree plus BOC exam.

questions.question: A Grade II ankle sprain involves:
options: Partial tear with moderate pain and swelling
questions.answer: Partial tear with moderate pain and swelling
questions.explanation: Grade II sprains involve partial tearing with moderate pain, swelling, and some instability.

questions.question: The anterior drawer test assesses the:
questions.explanation: The anterior drawer test stresses the ATFL, the most commonly injured lateral ankle ligament.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Blood doping involves:" is: Using anti-inflammatories.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Artificially increasing red blood cells to boost endurance.

questions.question: Graduated return to play after concussion requires:
options: Full contact return when symptoms resolve that day
Each step at least 24 hours apart, regression if symptoms return
questions.answer: Each step at least 24 hours apart, regression if symptoms return
questions.explanation: The graduated protocol ensures safe stepwise return to play.

questions.question: Stress fractures most commonly occur in:
questions.explanation: Repetitive stress exceeding bone remodeling capacity causes stress fractures in weight-bearing bones.

questions.question: HIPAA protects:
options: An AT's right to refuse treatment
questions.explanation: HIPAA mandates protection of patients' private health information.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Prophylactic ankle taping primarily prevents:" is: Excessive inversion leading to sprains.
questions.explanation: True. Excessive inversion leading to sprains is correct.

questions.question: A deload week is designed to:
options: Reduce volume and intensity for recovery and supercompensation
questions.answer: Reduce volume and intensity for recovery and supercompensation
questions.explanation: Deload weeks reduce accumulated fatigue, allowing the body to supercompensate beyond previous levels.

questions.question: An Emergency Action Plan ensures:
options: Rapid, coordinated response to life-threatening emergencies
questions.answer: Rapid, coordinated response to life-threatening emergencies
questions.explanation: EAPs define roles, communication protocols, and equipment locations for emergencies.

questions.question: Optimal post-exercise nutrition window is within:
questions.explanation: The 30–60 minute post-exercise window maximizes glycogen resynthesis and muscle protein synthesis.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "The SAID principle states the body:" is: Adapts randomly.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Adapts specifically to the demands placed on it.

questions.question: Proving negligence in athletic training does NOT require:
options: Duty of care owed to the athlete
Causal link between breach and harm
questions.explanation: Negligence is proven by duty, breach, causation, and damages — intent is irrelevant.

questions.question: IT band syndrome most commonly affects:
questions.explanation: Repetitive knee flexion-extension friction of the IT band over the lateral femoral condyle causes IT band syndrome.

questions.question: Creatine monohydrate is most effective for:
questions.explanation: Creatine enhances phosphocreatine resynthesis, improving brief maximal efforts.
